Zion National Park rangers reported multiple large waterfalls throughout the park. Additionally, the river gage on the Virgin River at Virgin recorded an increase in flows from 77 cfs to 1604 cfs between 1200PM and 100PM MDT.
Park rangers report 1ft flood wave on Sulphur Creek and 4ft flood wave on adjacent drainages.
Park rangers report large flood wave from Capitol Gorge leading to a road closure of the scenic drive in the area likely to last multiple days.
East Fork of the Virgin River gauge near Springdale observed a flood wave up to 1170 cfs.
Zion NP ranger reported multiple waterfalls through southern portion of the park, the biggest of which was noted near Twin Brothers.

North Fork of the Virgin River at Springdale river gage reported a flood wave of 883 cfs at 600 PM MDT. This was an increase from 64 cfs the previous hour. Additionally, Zion NP staff reported several waterfalls around 530 PM MDT.
Sulphur Creek flash flood reached the Visitors Center at 225 PM. Fremont is out of banks near the Fruita Campground. Evacuations are in progress.
Park Service reports ongoing flash flooding at Refrigerator and Echo Canyons.
Escalante River at Escalante Gauge up to 583 cfs.
ASOS station KSLC Salt Lake City Intl Arpt.
Mesonet station UTRKY Rocky Ridge.
Many areas of flooding in Cedar City, particularly on the north side of town. The sheriffs office reports multiple people trapped in their cars due to water on I-15 on the north side of town.
Mesonet station FREUT Fremont Island - Miller Hill.

Reports of flooding at a campground in Cedar Canyon. Cedar Canyon stream gauge reporting a significant rise.
798 cfs rise on the East Fork of the Virgin River on gauge ESTU1.

Mesonet station WDYPK Windy Peak. Elevation 10662 feet.
Debris flow due to heavy rain at the Bear Burn Scar. The flow remained internal to the scar and did not cross US-6, although there were a few softball-sized rocks that made it onto the roadway.
High flows from Halfway Hill burn scar into Pine Creek. Clogged culvert in Virginia Hills which caused flow to go into people's backyards.
Law enforcement reported Southern Parkway had extensive flooding. All flights from the St George Regional Airport were diverted for a time. Time is estimated.
Fort Pearce Wash Gauge (FPWU1) reported a flood wave over 400 cfs.

A flash flood was observed on Wahweap Creek. Time approximate.
Dirty Devil Gauge reported a rise over 700 CFS.
Flooding reported in the town of Bicknell. Water flowing across streets.
Report of debris with mud in Huntington Creek resulting from flow from the Trail Mountain Burn Scar.
Debris Flow on Cougar Run Road resulting from the Pole Creek/Bald Mountain Burn Scar. Reported by Media/Sheriffs office. Time estimated from radar.
Flash flood reported in Capitol Gorge and the Grand Wash.
Debris from East Fork Burn Scar crossed Rock Creek Road below Upper Stillwater Reservoir.
Multiple photos shared with media of landspout tornado somewhere east of Snowville. Time and location estimated from radar.
Local media and spotters reported a large tree was snapped on Main Street in St. George between 100S and 200S. Time estimated by radar.

East Fork of the Virgin River Gauge is at 530 cfs and rising.
Flash flood crossing streets in Hildale with debris, overflowing culverts.
Fort Pearce Wash flood wave up to 1300 cfs reported at gauge.
Debris flow over highway 6 due to heavy rain over the Bear burn scar.
Park Rangers reported waterfalls occurring along Streaked Wall as you get on Scenic Drive.

Broadcast media shared public video of a landspout tornado in an open field located northwest of the town of Clarkston. At this time there is no known damage in the area. The observer reported the time to be approximately 430PM. Radar imagery at the time showed a thunderstorm in the area with broad weak rotation along an outflow boundary.
Photos on social media of a landspout tornado north of the town of Roosevelt. Location/time estimated from the photos and viewing angle.

Debris flow across State Highway 24 making it impassible just east of the Capitol Reef Visitor Center between Hickman Bridge and Grand Wash. In addition a flash flood observed on Sulphur Creek, and high flows on Grand Wash.

Grand Staircase Visitor Center in Kanab reported flash flooding in town. Local streets flooded and covered with mud and debris, temporarily closing a few streets. Creeks through town overflowed at culverts across the roads. One spot in particular, Kanab Creek overflowed 9-12 inches deep across the road. Dirt roads on the outskirts of town were impassable Wed evening. In addition Kane County Sheriff reported several homes had some minor basement flooding.

Hildale Fire Dispatch reported quarter size hail lasting about 10 minutes. Windshields were broken, vegetation was shredded, and hail accumulated up to 3 inches deep in some spots. Flash Flooding also occurred.

Flash flooding of Short Creek and its tributaries through Hildale/Colorado City. All creek road crossings were closed for 2 to 3 hours. The Hildale Street creek crossing was heavily damaged and will be closed until repairs can be made. Severe hail also occurred.

Grand Staircase-Escalante National Monument at Big Water reported a flash flood wave on Wahweap Creek that was audible from the town of Big Water around 9 PM. Surveying the creek at Smoky Mountain Road low-water crossing the next morning revealed the water was 60-75 feet wide at some point overnight, with this width corresponding to 3-4 feet of depth at this point, which would make the road impassible to even high clearance vehicles. Per radar, heavy rain fell in the far headwaters between about 4PM and 6PM.
Photos from social media show a large tree snapped in half in St. George. Exact location unknown. Time estimated.
